title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: Strict phase order; Phase N+1 work Phase N exit criteria green. Layer order sacred: Layer 1 (core) fully built tested Layer 2 (grobs), built Layer 3 (geoms). geom may never implement roughening logic directly — Layer 1. “Done” = code + roxygen + tests + cited invariants passing + zero-NOTE devtools::check() + checkbox ticked commit reference.

title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: R1 Zero-NOTE R CMD check ---cran every checkbox commit; Imports grow per BUILD_PLAN phase manifest. NeedsCompilation: v1 (C/C++; cpp11 post-v1 spike ). R2 Clean-room implementation (license posture, ADR-0003): never vendor, copy, line--line translate rough.js / hachure-fill / points--path source. Reimplement published algorithms (PRD §2, sourced https://shihn.ca/posts/2020/roughjs-algorithms/) original R. JavaScript ships. README + inst/NOTICE credit rough.js (MIT) Wood et al. inspiration; state non-affiliation. R3 Layer purity: Layer 1 (R/core-*.R) imports nothing ggplot2 grid performs drawing — maps numbers→numbers. drawing unit conversion lives Layer 2+. CI lint asserts grid::/ggplot2:: symbols R/core-*.R (T-ARCH-01). R4 Roughen inch space, never data space. point displacement dampening happen conversion absolute units inside makeContent(). geom roughens native/data units defect (T-CORE-05). R5 Determinism: every randomized routine draws seeded local RNG stream via withr::with_seed() (explicit stream); must restore never mutate user’s global .Random.seed (T-CORE-06). bare runif/rnorm package code. R6 Public ggplot2 API : extend via ggproto/Geom, draw_panel/draw_group, coord$transform, draw_key, documented layer() construction. Never touch private internals (layout$panel_params shape, etc.) without guarded shim version check; CI tests ggplot2 3.5 4.0. R7 linewidth size line widths (3.4.0 convention); points use size. Match ggplot2’s default_aes conventions exactly layers compose. R8 Snapshot discipline: PRIMARY regression gate deterministic Layer-1 geometry snapshots (numbers), images. vdiffr image snapshots secondary, fixed-seed, tolerance-tuned. Update either SNAPSHOT-UPDATE: commit trailer explaining intended visual change. R9 Never edit generated docs (man/, NAMESPACE); edit roxygen run devtools::document(). R10 Upstream claims need provenance: algorithmic API claim specs/ADRs cites source URL marked UNVERIFIED:. PRD §7 list standing source set. ggplot2 4.0 behavior uncertain, write probe tools/probes/ record observed behavior (“observed ggplot2 X.Y.Z”), never “documented.”

title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: R: tidyverse style; rlang/cli argument checking + errors (cli::cli_abort); one geom family per file; @family roxygen groups; runnable @examples; constructors follow canonical geom_*(mapping, data, stat, position, ..., na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes) shape. Layer 1 functions plain, side-effect-free, fully typed docs, individually benchmarkable. Naming: geoms geom_sketch_<thing>; params roughness, bowing, n_passes, fill_style, hachure_angle, hachure_gap, fill_weight, seed; theme theme_sketch(); core fns roughen_*, rough_*, hachure_fill.

title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: CI runs suite twice: ggplot2 3.5.x release, 4.0.x.
code: Rscript -e 'devtools::document(); devtools::test()' Rscript -e 'devtools::check(args = "--as-cran")' Rscript -e 'vdiffr::manage_cases()'        # review image snapshots Rscript tools/bench/run.R                   # AC-8 harness Rscript tools/probes/ggplot2-4-panelparams.R   # version-behavior probe

title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: specs/<name>.md completed + reviewed implementing: roughen.md (line/ellipse/curve math + dampening), hachure.md (scan-line AET, angle rotation, derived styles), geom-contract.md (shared default_aes/param/draw_key contract every geom follows). spec cites source section implements (R10) lists fixture cases.

title: DEVELOPMENT.md — contributor guide for {ggsketch}
text: Stop write open DECISIONS.md entry : R2 (license posture) strained; probe shows ggplot2 4.0 needs private internals; concave-fill correctness can’t meet AC-5 chosen algorithm; dependency seems unavoidable; acceptance criterion unmeasurable written.

title: Controlling the sketch
text: far points displaced. 0 ruler-straight; default 1; values ~3 get loose scribbly.  bars:
code: x  <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 50) d  <- data.frame(x = x, y = sin(x))  ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "0.0  (straight)"), roughness = 0,   seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "1.0  (default)"),  roughness = 1,   seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "2.5"),             roughness = 2.5, seed = 2) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(colour = "5.0  (loose)"),    roughness = 5,   seed = 2) +   scale_colour_brewer("roughness", palette = "Set1") +   labs(title = "The roughness dial") +   theme_sketch() df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) for (r in c(0, 1, 3)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", roughness = r, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("roughness =", r), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: much line segments bow (curve) endpoints. Higher values give springier, relaxed stroke.
code: seg <- data.frame(x = 1, y = 1, xend = 6, yend = 4) for (b in c(0, 2, 5)) {   print(     ggplot(seg) +       geom_sketch_segment(aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                           bowing = b, roughness = 0.6, linewidth = 1,                           seed = 7L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("bowing =", b)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: many overlaid strokes draw. 2 (default) gives classic “double-stroke” sketch look; 1 single pass; 3+ looks heavily worked.
code: for (n in c(1, 2, 4)) {   print(     ggplot(d, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_line(n_passes = n, roughness = 1.5, linewidth = 0.7,                        colour = "grey20", seed = 3L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("n_passes =", n)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: sketch random, seeded. given seed always produces drawing, figures reproducible across renders devices. Change seed fresh “hand”.  first two panels identical (seed); third differs.
code: d2 <- data.frame(x = 1:8, y = c(2, 5, 3, 8, 6, 9, 7, 10)) for (s in c(1, 1, 42)) {   print(     ggplot(d2, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_line(linewidth = 1, seed = s) +       geom_sketch_point(size = 3, seed = s + 100) +       labs(subtitle = paste("seed =", s)) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Controlling the sketch
text: pass seed, geoms use getOption("ggsketch.seed", 1L). Set make whole document reproducible: ggsketch never touches global random state: randomized routine draws seeded stream restores .Random.seed afterward, interfere set.seed() elsewhere analysis.
code: options(ggsketch.seed = 1L)

title: Fill styles in depth
code: styles <- c("hachure", "cross_hatch", "zigzag", "zigzag_line",             "dots", "dashed", "solid")  bars <- do.call(rbind, lapply(styles, function(s) {   data.frame(style = s, x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) })) bars$style <- factor(bars$style, levels = styles)  # Draw a small panel per style by faceting and re-drawing each with its style. # (fill_style is a layer parameter, so we build one layer per facet.) plots <- lapply(styles, function(s) {   ggplot(subset(bars, style == s), aes(x, y)) +     geom_sketch_col(fill = "#E8A87C", fill_style = s, seed = 4L) +     labs(subtitle = s, x = NULL, y = NULL) +     theme_sketch(base_size = 9) })  # Show them individually: for (p in plots) print(p)

title: Fill styles in depth
text: Three parameters control texture.

title: Fill styles in depth
text: angle (degrees) fill lines:
code: df <- data.frame(x = c("A", "B", "C"), y = c(4, 6, 3)) for (a in c(0, 45, 90, 135)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "seagreen", hachure_angle = a, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("hachure_angle =", a), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: Spacing fill lines, data units — smaller means denser shading:
code: for (g in c(0.6, 1.2, 2.4)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#5DADE2", hachure_gap = g, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("hachure_gap =", g), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: stroke weight fill lines:
code: for (w in c(0.3, 0.8, 1.6)) {   print(     ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +       geom_sketch_col(fill = "#AF7AC5", fill_weight = w, seed = 1L) +       labs(subtitle = paste("fill_weight =", w), x = NULL) +       theme_sketch(base_size = 9)   ) }

title: Fill styles in depth
text: filler true scan-line algorithm, texture conforms polygon outline, just rectangles:
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 9)[-9] hex  <- data.frame(x = cos(ang), y = sin(ang)) ggplot(hex, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "#E74C3C", fill_style = "cross_hatch",                       hachure_angle = 30, seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Cross-hatch on a hexagon") +   theme_sketch() td <- expand.grid(x = 1:5, y = 1:3) td$z <- td$x ggplot(td, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_sketch_tile(fill_style = "dots", seed = 1L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "Dotted tiles") +   theme_sketch()

title: Fill styles in depth
text: fill geometry comes pure Layer-1 functions can call — useful testing custom grobs. sketch_fill() dispatches styles returns list line segments:
code: square_x <- c(0, 1, 1, 0) square_y <- c(0, 0, 1, 1) segs <- sketch_fill(square_x, square_y, fill_style = "hachure",                     hachure_gap = 0.15, seed = 1L) length(segs)      # number of fill segments #> [1] 8 head(segs[[1]])   # each segment is a matrix of x/y points #>              x          y #> [1,] 0.7093499 0.07633409 #> [2,] 0.8440814 0.20310311 #> [3,] 0.9270041 0.27937282

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_col() draws roughened outline hachure (pencil-shading) fill.  Map fill variable like ggplot2 bar. bar gets seed offset, two bars wobble identically.  geom_sketch_bar() counts rows (like geom_bar()):  Bars flip stack just like originals:
code: sales <- data.frame(product = c("Alpha", "Bravo", "Charlie", "Delta", "Echo"),                     units   = c(34, 51, 22, 47, 39))  ggplot(sales, aes(product, units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#7BAFD4", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Units sold", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(sales, aes(product, units, fill = product)) +   geom_sketch_col(seed = 2L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Mapped fill", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(class)) +   geom_sketch_bar(fill = "#C39BD3", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "Vehicle count by class", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) ggplot(sales, aes(reorder(product, units), units)) +   geom_sketch_col(fill = "#F1948A", seed = 1L) +   coord_flip() +   labs(title = "Horizontal bars", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_histogram() bins continuous variable draws hand-drawn bars; geom_sketch_freqpoly() draws counts roughened line.
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions)) +   geom_sketch_histogram(fill = "#7BAFD4", bins = 20, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruption times") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(hwy, colour = drv)) +   geom_sketch_freqpoly(bins = 15, linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Highway mpg by drivetrain", x = "hwy") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_point() draws point small roughened ellipse:  Lines points compose like layers:  Multiple groups, one seed per group:
code: econ <- economics[economics$date > as.Date("2000-01-01"), ] ggplot(econ, aes(date, unemploy)) +   geom_sketch_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "US unemployment", x = NULL, y = "thousands") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 4, seed = 1L) +   scale_colour_brewer("cylinders", palette = "Dark2") +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs weight") +   theme_sketch() df <- data.frame(x = 1:12, y = c(3, 5, 4, 7, 6, 9, 8, 11, 9, 12, 11, 14)) ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_line(colour = "grey40", seed = 3L) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 4, colour = "firebrick", seed = 8L) +   labs(title = "Trend with markers") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(ggplot2::economics_long, aes(date, value01, colour = variable)) +   geom_sketch_line(seed = 5L) +   labs(title = "Five series, hand-drawn", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: size behaves like ggplot2 point size. Set constant bigger smaller markers:  map size variable bubble chart — pair scale_size_area() area (radius) encodes value:  small-multiples sweep single size aesthetic:
code: sz <- data.frame(x = 1:5, y = 1, s = c(2, 4, 6, 9, 13)) ggplot(sz, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_point(size = sz$s, colour = "#2E86C1", seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Fixed point sizes (2 → 13)", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, size = hp, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_sketch_point(alpha = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   scale_size_area("horsepower", max_size = 14) +   scale_colour_brewer("cylinders", palette = "Dark2") +   labs(title = "Bubble chart: size = horsepower") +   theme_sketch() grid <- expand.grid(x = 1:6, y = 1:3) grid$s <- seq(1.5, 11, length.out = nrow(grid)) ggplot(grid, aes(x, y, size = s)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "#884EA0", show.legend = FALSE, seed = 3L) +   scale_size_identity() +   labs(title = "Increasing point size", x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_jitter() spreads overplotted points; geom_sketch_count() sizes single point many observations sit .
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0, colour = "#5D6D7E",                      size = 2, seed = 1L) +   labs(title = "Jittered highway mpg", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) ggplot(mpg, aes(cty, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_count(colour = "#C0392B", seed = 2L) +   scale_size_area(max_size = 8) +   labs(title = "Overplot count") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: sketchy heatmap geom_sketch_tile():  sketchy 2-D bin heatmap geom_sketch_bin2d() (cells default hachure fill, shaded count):  geom_sketch_hex() bins hexagons instead (needs hexbin package):
code: rects <- data.frame(xmin = c(1, 3, 5), xmax = c(2, 4, 6),                     ymin = 0, ymax = c(2, 4, 3)) ggplot(rects) +   geom_sketch_rect(aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax,                        fill = factor(xmin)),                    seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_rect()") +   theme_sketch() td <- expand.grid(x = 1:8, y = 1:6) td$z <- td$x + td$y ggplot(td, aes(x, y, fill = z)) +   geom_sketch_tile(seed = 2L, hachure_gap = 0.18) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_tile()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_bin2d(bins = 12, seed = 3L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_bin2d()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_sketch_hex(bins = 12, seed = 4L) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma") +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_hex()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Concave polygons fill correctly (hachure respects every notch):
code: ang  <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 11)[-11] r    <- rep(c(1, 0.45), length.out = 10) star <- data.frame(x = r * cos(ang), y = r * sin(ang)) ggplot(star, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_polygon(fill = "tomato", seed = 1L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "A concave star") +   theme_sketch() band <- data.frame(x = 1:20) band$y  <- 10 + 5 * sin(seq(0, 3 * pi, length.out = 20)) band$lo <- band$y - 2 band$hi <- band$y + 2  ggplot(band, aes(x)) +   geom_sketch_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), fill = "plum", seed = 2L) +   geom_sketch_line(aes(y = y), seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "Ribbon + line") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(band, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_area(fill = "lightgreen", seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_area()") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions)) +   geom_sketch_density(fill = "khaki", seed = 4L) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruptions") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_violin() mirrors kernel density closed polygon hachure-fills .
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(class, hwy, fill = class)) +   geom_sketch_violin(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +   labs(title = "Highway mpg distribution by class", x = NULL) +   theme_sketch() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

title: Gallery — every geom
text: hand-drawn fit roughened confidence band:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Linear fit with CI band") +   theme_sketch() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey50", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_smooth(seed = 2L, colour = "darkorange") +   labs(title = "loess fit") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_function() sketches analytic curve x range, example overlay theoretical density.
code: ggplot(data.frame(x = c(-4, 4)), aes(x)) +   geom_sketch_function(fun = dnorm, colour = "#2E86C1", linewidth = 0.9,                        seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(sd = 1.6),                        colour = "#C0392B", linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Two normal densities", y = "density") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_qq() draws quantile-quantile points geom_sketch_qq_line() reference line. Map data sample aesthetic.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(sample = mpg)) +   geom_sketch_qq(size = 2.5, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_qq_line(colour = "#C8553D", linewidth = 0.8, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "Normal Q-Q plot of mpg", x = "theoretical", y = "sample") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_quantile() fits draws quantile regression lines (requires optional quantreg package).
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy)) +   geom_sketch_point(colour = "grey60", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_quantile(quantiles = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), colour = "#6C3483",                        linewidth = 0.9, seed = 2L) +   labs(title = "10th / 50th / 90th percentile fits") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: Radii data units, use coord_equal() true circles:
code: cdf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 3, 2), y = c(1, 1, 2.5),                   r = c(0.6, 0.9, 0.5), grp = c("a", "b", "c")) ggplot(cdf, aes(x, y, r = r, fill = grp)) +   geom_sketch_circle(seed = 1L, show.legend = FALSE) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_circle()") +   theme_sketch() edf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 3), y = c(1, 2), a = c(1.4, 0.8), b = c(0.6, 1.2)) ggplot(edf, aes(x, y, a = a, b = b, fill = factor(x))) +   geom_sketch_ellipse(seed = 2L, show.legend = FALSE) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_ellipse()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
code: sdf <- data.frame(x = 1:4, y = c(1, 3, 2, 4),                   xend = 2:5, yend = c(3, 1, 4, 2)) ggplot(sdf) +   geom_sketch_segment(aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),                       colour = "darkgreen", linewidth = 1, seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_segment()") +   theme_sketch() stp <- data.frame(x = 1:8, y = c(1, 3, 2, 5, 4, 6, 5, 8)) ggplot(stp, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_step(colour = "purple", linewidth = 1, seed = 4L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 5L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_step()") +   theme_sketch()

title: Gallery — every geom
text: geom_sketch_curve() curved connector (quadratic Bézier); curvature sets much bends.  geom_sketch_spoke() draws segment point angle radius — useful vector fields.
code: cdf <- data.frame(x = c(1, 1, 1), y = c(1, 2, 3),                   xend = c(4, 4, 4), yend = c(1, 2, 3)) ggplot(cdf, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_curve(aes(xend = xend, yend = yend), curvature = 0.4,                     colour = "#1A5276", linewidth = 0.9, seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(seed = 2L) +   geom_sketch_point(aes(x = xend, y = yend), seed = 3L) +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_curve()") +   theme_sketch() field <- expand.grid(x = 1:6, y = 1:6) field$angle  <- with(field, atan2(y - 3.5, x - 3.5)) field$radius <- 0.6 ggplot(field, aes(x, y)) +   geom_sketch_spoke(aes(angle = angle, radius = radius),                     colour = "#117A65", seed = 1L) +   geom_sketch_point(size = 1.5, seed = 2L) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "geom_sketch_spoke()") +   theme_sketch()

title: How it works
text: core-* functions map numbers numbers. take coordinates sketch parameters return roughened coordinates — grid, ggplot2, drawing. rough.js algorithms reimplemented original R: point roughening, cubic-Bézier sampling, ellipse generation, Active-Edge-Table scan-line filler hachure (including concave polygons). can call directly: Layer 1 pure, package’s primary regression tests deterministic geometry snapshots — compare numbers exactly, far stable comparing rendered images.
code: # Two passes of a roughened polyline through three points passes <- roughen_polyline(x = c(0, 1, 2), y = c(0, 1, 0),                            roughness = 1, n_passes = 2, seed = 1L) length(passes)       # one matrix per pass #> [1] 2 head(passes[[1]])    # x/y coordinates of the first stroke #>                 x          y #> [1,] -0.002152691 0.02202165 #> [2,]  0.117443112 0.12637195 #> [3,]  0.225590483 0.22216896 #> [4,]  0.323171774 0.31015873 #> [5,]  0.411069335 0.39108729 #> [6,]  0.490165519 0.46570069 # Hachure fill of a unit square: a list of line segments segs <- hachure_fill(c(0, 1, 1, 0), c(0, 0, 1, 1),                      hachure_gap = 0.2, hachure_angle = 45, seed = 1L) length(segs) #> [1] 6

text: *_grob() constructors wrap Layer 1 grid grobs whose makeContent() method drawing. title: How it works
text: Every randomized routine draws seeded local RNG stream restores global random state afterwards. sketch plots reproducible given seed, never disturb set.seed() elsewhere code:
code: set.seed(99) before <- runif(1)  # draw something sketchy (consumes randomness internally) invisible(roughen_polyline(c(0, 1), c(0, 1), seed = 1L))  set.seed(99) after <- runif(1) identical(before, after)   # TRUE — global RNG untouched #> [1] TRUE

title: ggsketch — hand-drawn geoms for ggplot2
text: Three layers, kept strictly separate: Layer 1 — pure geometry (R/core-*.R): numbers → numbers. Seeded roughening, ellipse/Bézier sampling, Active-Edge-Table scan-line hachure filler handles concave polygons. grid, ggplot2. Layer 2 — grid grobs (R/grob-*.R): makeContent() converts device inches re-roughens real render size, resizing re-draws cleanly. Layer 3 — ggproto geoms (R/geom-*.R): standard ggplot2 extension API. Roughening always happens inch space, look consistent across aspect ratios devices.

what: ggsketch: Grammar-Native Hand-Drawn Geoms for ggplot2 — ggsketch-package
title: ggsketch: Grammar-Native Hand-Drawn Geoms for ggplot2 — ggsketch-package
text: Provides ggplot2 geoms render hand-drawn / sketchy aesthetic — roughened strokes, double-pass lines, hachure / cross-hatch / zigzag / dots fill patterns. Implemented pure-R grid grobs wrapped first-class ggproto geoms, composable aes(), stats, scales, faceting. Works every R graphics device (PDF/PNG/SVG/screen) browser dependency. Algorithms reimplemented published rough.js algorithm description (Shihn, 2020) Wood et al. (2012) — see inst/NOTICE attribution.

title: ggsketch 1.4.0
text: frame can now roughened , plus matching colour palette, new fill style, reproducible fonts. Rough theme elements. element_sketch_line() element_sketch_rect() render gridlines, panel borders, axis ticks roughened sketch grobs. theme_sketch(rough_frame = TRUE) turns frame matches marks. Works ggplot2 3.5 4.0 (S3 element_grob methods; sketch params stored attributes survive theme merging). Sketch colour scales. scale_colour_sketch() / scale_fill_sketch() use qualitative palette (sketch_palette()) chosen suit hand-drawn look; *_sketch_c() variants give continuous blue gradient. New fill style "scribble" — one continuous winding stroke overshoots boundary, like scribbling fill shape. Available everywhere fill_style accepted. Reproducible fonts. register_sketch_font() registers handwriting font file (via systemfonts) result reproduces machine CI runner without system install; font resolver now also finds registered fonts.

title: ggsketch 1.3.0
text: New geoms (Tier 3, first batch — 2-D density text): geom_sketch_contour() — hand-drawn contour lines surface (via stat_contour). geom_sketch_density2d() / geom_sketch_density_2d() — 2-D kernel-density contour lines (via stat_density_2d). geom_sketch_hex() — hexagonal binning heatmap (needs optional hexbin package). geom_sketch_text() geom_sketch_label() — text handwriting font (sketch text font, roughened glyphs). now honour nudge_x/nudge_y like ggplot2::geom_text(), font resolver falls back handwriting faces preinstalled Windows/macOS (Segoe Print, Ink Free, Bradley Hand, Chalkboard, Comic Sans MS, …) sketchy face found even Caveat installed. Use font-aware device (ragg, svglite, cairo) render handwriting font; base GDI png() device may fall back default family. Still come Tier 3: filled contour / 2-D density bands (*_filled, need multi-ring hole-aware hachure) geom_sketch_dotplot().

title: ggsketch 1.2.0
text: New geoms (Tier 2 coverage — stats connectors; reuse existing grobs, one new optional dependency): geom_sketch_count() — points sized overplot count (via stat_sum). geom_sketch_function() — sketch function curve (via stat_function). geom_sketch_qq() geom_sketch_qq_line() — quantile-quantile points reference line. geom_sketch_quantile() — quantile regression lines (needs optional quantreg package). geom_sketch_rug() — marginal ticks along panel edges (sides). geom_sketch_spoke() — segments (x, y) angle + radius. geom_sketch_curve() — hand-drawn curved connector (quadratic Bezier). geom_sketch_bin2d() / geom_sketch_bin_2d() — rectangular 2-D bin heatmap.

title: ggsketch 1.1.0
text: New geoms (Tier 1 coverage — reuse existing grobs base ggplot2 stats; new hard dependencies): geom_sketch_histogram() geom_sketch_freqpoly() — binned distributions (via stat_bin). geom_sketch_jitter() — geom_sketch_point() jitter position. geom_sketch_violin() — mirrored kernel density hachure fill (via stat_ydensity). Interval family: geom_sketch_linerange(), geom_sketch_pointrange(), geom_sketch_errorbar(), geom_sketch_crossbar(). Reference lines: geom_sketch_abline(), geom_sketch_hline(), geom_sketch_vline().

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: First public release. grammar-native, pure-R implementation rough.js hand-drawn aesthetic ggplot2 — JavaScript, works every graphics device.

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: Lines & points: geom_sketch_line(), geom_sketch_path(), geom_sketch_point(). Bars & tiles: geom_sketch_col(), geom_sketch_bar(), geom_sketch_rect(), geom_sketch_tile(). Areas & curves: geom_sketch_polygon() (concave-safe fill), geom_sketch_ribbon(), geom_sketch_area(), geom_sketch_density(), geom_sketch_smooth(). Circular & composed: geom_sketch_circle(), geom_sketch_ellipse(), geom_sketch_segment(), geom_sketch_step(), geom_sketch_boxplot(). Helpers: annotate_sketch() theme_sketch() (light + dark presets).

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: hachure, cross_hatch, zigzag, zigzag_line, dots, dashed, solid, via full Active-Edge-Table scan-line filler handles concave polygons.

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: Three strict layers: pure geometry (Layer 1), grid grobs makeContent() (Layer 2), ggproto geoms (Layer 3). Roughening happens device-inch space. Reproducible: every randomized routine draws seeded local RNG stream never mutates user’s global .Random.seed. Set options(ggsketch.seed=). Sketchy legend keys every geom.

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: Algorithms reimplemented original R published descriptions; rough.js source included (see inst/NOTICE). Independent , affiliated , rough.js ggrough.

title: ggsketch 1.0.0
text: 0.0.1 — Layer 1 sketch core (roughen, ellipse, Bézier, hachure, fill styles). 0.1.0 — Layer 2 grobs + first geoms (line/path/point) + theme_sketch(). 0.2.0 — Filled rectangular geoms (col/bar/rect/tile) + fill-style coverage. 0.3.0 — Polygons, areas, ribbons, density, smooth. 1.0.0 — Circular & composed geoms, annotations, theming, docs, release.
